claims.statement: On this machine and the recorded five-million-cell numeric targeted-upsert workload, the schema-v6 relationship scanner consumed one 167-byte metadata batch while the transform staged 183697577 worksheet bytes; median scanner time was 3 microseconds, the owned output buffer remained 262144 bytes and median process peak working set was 8.25781 MB.

On the recorded one-million-cell formula workload with an external A1 hyperlink, the scanner consumed one 303-byte metadata batch, visited two relationship/namespace slow-path tags and recorded an 8-microsecond median; the representative output opened with openpyxl 3.1.2 and preserved the recorded hyperlink target.

Every warm-up and measured output passed package byte accounting and exact compressed-payload checks for each planned raw-copy entry. Microsoft Office was not run, and system-load-sensitive total elapsed times are not used as improvement claims.

The implementation passed focused relationship/range tests, production and stored-only 132-test CTest profiles, the no-images runtime smoke and installed-package consumer builds for production, stored-only and no-images profiles.
